mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60d returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output that compresses corporate earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of contraction periods, or the signal fails to predict direction during tightening phases (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and the consumer price index indicates real rate compression that boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate spread loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) or yields negative returns in >55% of observations during disinflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll declines coincide with positive 60d returns in >60% of cases, or rolling out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.01 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins and triggers positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of high-growth quarters, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for market volatility (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable employment levels signals efficient capacity utilization that supports earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases while PAYEMS growth remains positive
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20d returns in >60% of recovery periods, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance (Rank IC < 0.0) in walk-forward tests.
